items.title: Taiwan’s Yang Cautions Against Borrowing to Buy Red-Hot Stocks
items.description: Taiwan’s central bank chief has warned investors of the risk of borrowing to invest in the island’s red-hot stocks, which have been surging on global demand for tech products at the heart of the AI bo

items.title: Seven & i Raises Profit Outlook, Bolstering Turnaround Hopes
items.description: Seven & i Holdings Co. lifted its profit and sales outlook for the current fiscal year, raising hopes that the convenience-store operator’s turnaround efforts are taking hold.

items.title: Lenders Seek $700 Million After Japan Payments Firm’s Collapse
items.description: Creditors across Japan are trying to claw back more than $700 million from a payments firm suspected to have faked its financial statement for at least 20 years and abruptly collapsed into bankruptcy

items.title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/9/2026
items.description: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews,

items.title: Malaysia Says It Still Has to Pay $2 Billion in 1MDB Debts
items.description: The Malaysian government must pay 8.9 billion ringgit ($2.18 billion) in remaining debts it incurred from the 1Malaysia Development Berhad scandal, Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said on Thurs

items.title: FTSE 100 Misses Out on Rebound as Astra Slumps
Pakistan Seeks Urgent LNG Cargo as Hormuz Attacks Disrupt Supply
items.description: Pakistan is urgently seeking to purchase a shipment of liquefied natural gas after fresh hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz again disrupted supplies.

items.title: Tata Group Targets $100 Billion Revenue from Auto Units by 2031
items.description: Tata Group is targeting about $100 billion in automotive revenue by the year ended March 2031, Chairman Natarajan Chandrasekaran said, outlining growth plans across its four automotive businesses foll

items.title: Deutsche Bank Eyes India, Indonesia Bonds If Oil Holds Below $70
items.description: Deutsche Bank AG’s private arm will consider buying emerging Asia bonds if oil prices stay low enough to push down inflation and yields.

items.title: Jupiter Fund Ditches US Treasuries in Favor of European Bonds
items.description: Jupiter Asset Management has cut US Treasury holdings to zero in one of its main bond funds, buying European government notes and adding to its hefty emerging-markets position instead.

items.title: Australia Agrees to Sell Uranium to India During Modi Visit
items.description: Australia has agreed to sell uranium to India for power generation in a landmark deal signed between the two nations during a visit to Melbourne by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

items.title: China’s Top Solar Firm Makes Switch From Silver to Copper
items.description: China’s biggest solar maker has begun producing cells that replace silver with copper, in response to soaring prices of the precious metal that have rattled the industry since last year.

items.title: Modi Kicks Off Two-Day Australia Visit
items.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese pledge to deepen economic and defense ties during talks in Melbourne. The two leaders are expected to sign agreements

items.title: Hormuz Traffic Grinds to Near Halt After US, Iran Strike
items.description: The US military struck Iran for the second straight day, an escalation of violence that threatens efforts to reach a permanent peace deal. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Goldman Says Hormuz Flare-Up May Delay Recovery in Oil Supplies
items.description: A recovery in Middle Eastern oil supplies could be set back if renewed tensions disrupt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. said.

items.title: China Asks Bankers to Avoid Underwriting Short-Term LGFV Bonds
items.description: China is steering municipal borrowers away from issuing short-term bonds in the domestic market, according to people familiar with the matter, in its latest bid to curb fundraising by weaker issuers.

items.title: The Memorandum of Misunderstanding’s Margin of Error
items.description: Markets have a situation they can live with as long as one big line isn’t crossed.

items.title: Temasek CIO Rohit Sipahimalani Warns on US Capex Surge Risks
items.description: Temasek International CIO Rohit Sipahimalani discusses investment opportunities and warns that a surge in US capital spending could create risks for markets in a wide-ranging interview with Bloomberg'

items.title: Japan’s Five-Year Bond Sale Sees Decent Demand on Higher Yields
items.description: Demand at Japan’s five-year bond sale on Thursday was broadly in line with recent auctions, showing that elevated yields are attracting some buyers.

items.title: Hormuz Ship Traffic Grinds to a Near Halt After US, Iran Strikes
items.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a near standstill on Thursday, after the US struck Iran for a second straight day as a fragile truce between the two sides looked increasingly shaky.
